question: Is the mechanical owl graffiti from the donor image clearly visible on the wall?
Does the graffiti retain the key design elements of the reference owl, such as the gear-shaped eyes, circuit-board wing patterns, and blue/orange color scheme?
Is the graffiti positioned specifically within the central, recessed rectangular section of the wall (the bricked-up window area)?
Does the graffiti appear to be painted directly onto the bricks, allowing the rough texture and bumps of the masonry to remain visible through the paint?
Does the graffiti show realistic interruptions, shadowing, or conformity to the depth of the mortar joints between the bricks?
Are the edges of the graffiti slightly soft, diffused, or splattered, mimicking a spray-paint application rather than a sharp digital cutout?
Are the colors of the graffiti slightly muted or influenced by the underlying red brick tone, avoiding an unnaturally bright or glossy 'sticker' appearance?
Is the image free from artificial drop shadows behind the owl that would imply it is a separate object floating in front of the wall?
Are the vines framing the central brick section preserved intact, without being erased or overwritten by the graffiti?
If any vine tendrils cross into the graffiti area, does the paint appear behind the vines (proper occlusion) rather than on top of them?
Is the brick pattern and structural geometry of the wall outside the graffiti area identical to the original image?
Is the image free from rectangular borders, white canvas backgrounds, or halo artifacts around the graffiti?
